subject: Product Management
title: Discovery vs Delivery
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Discovery vs Delivery.

hook.text: Product work has two halves: discovery (deciding what to build) and delivery (building it). Most teams skip the first.

buildup.text: Delivery is visible: sprints, standups, releases. Discovery is invisible: interviews, experiments, prototypes.

discovery.text: Teams that skip discovery ship on time but miss the target. Teams that invest in it ship less but ship right.

twist.text: The best product teams run discovery and delivery in parallel — always learning while always building.

climax.text: Discovery isn't a phase before development. It's a continuous practice that never stops.

punchline.text: Build and learn at the same time. Never just build.

quiz.question: How should discovery and delivery relate?
options: They should run in parallel — continuous learning while building
Discovery happens once before development starts
